text.title: Rate Limiting Failure Modes
text.summary: Antipatterns observed in real deployments of Rate Limiting. Each ties a symptom to a root cause and a concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: At 14:59:59 attacker burns 100 req; at 15:00:01 burns another 100 - effective 200 in 2 seconds.
antipattern.root-cause: Fixed_window algorithm chosen for burst-sensitive endpoint.
antipattern.fix: Switch to sliding_window_counter or token_bucket for auth/payment classes.

antipattern.symptom: Office of 200 employees all blocked together; one user shared egress IP.
antipattern.root-cause: Per-IP key without X-Forwarded-For handling behind LB.
antipattern.fix: Switch to user_id key; for unauthenticated endpoints use composite (ip + UA fingerprint) with higher limit.

antipattern.symptom: Clients hit 429 and retry every 100ms; service stays at 100% CPU.
antipattern.fix: Always emit Retry-After seconds = window remaining; document SDK retry policy.

antipattern.symptom: LB removes all pods from rotation; service down.
antipattern.root-cause: Per-IP limit applied to /healthz; LB IP exceeded budget.
antipattern.fix: Add /healthz + /metrics to bypass_paths with exact match.
